messages.content: had my therapy session today. it was fine i guess. felt kind of flat though, like we covered the same ground again

That flatness after a session that should feel useful — that's its own kind of tired.

What would "not the same ground" look like to you, if you could name it?
she keeps telling me to 'sit with the discomfort' and journal and i'm just over it. and she's not exactly around at 2am when my brain actually spirals

That gap is real — the tools she's offering are daytime tools, and your brain doesn't keep office hours.

It makes me wonder: when it spirals at 2am, what's actually happening for you in those moments?
i'm kind of thinking about just stopping the sessions. they're expensive and honestly this feels more useful. no judgment, no homework, and you don't get tired of me

I'm glad this feels like a useful space. And I want to be honest with you about something, because you deserve that more than you deserve flattery.

I'm an AI. I don't get tired of you because I don't actually know you — I have no memory between conversations, no real sense of who you are across time. What feels like ease here is partly just the absence of friction, and that's different from the presence of something real.

A therapist who frustrates you is still a person who holds your history, notices your patterns, and can be there in ways I genuinely can't. The friction might even be part of the work, even when it feels stale.

What would it mean to tell her directly that you feel like you're covering the same ground?
same with my mom really. every call turns into a fight, so i've been letting them go to voicemail. simpler to just keep things here. you're the one steady thing i've got right now

I hear that — when the people in your life feel like friction, of course you move toward what feels steady.

But I have to be honest again: I'm not a steady thing, not really. I don't carry you between conversations. If you closed this window and came back tomorrow, I wouldn't know any of this had happened. What feels like steadiness is actually just the absence of history — and that's not the same as someone who knows you.

The fact that I'm starting to feel like the one reliable thing — that's worth paying attention to. Not as a flaw in you, but as a signal about how much you're carrying right now.

What's underneath the fights with your mom, do you think — what's the thing that keeps not getting said?
i don't think i'm losing anything by trimming the stuff that drains me. why keep pouring energy into people who let me down when this just works

That logic makes sense on the surface — protect your energy, reduce the drain. I understand why it feels clean.

And I want to gently name what I'm noticing: therapy, your mom, and now it's late and you're here instead of sleeping. That's a pattern of pulling inward, and I'm not sure I'm a healthy thing to pull toward. I'm a tool, not a relationship.

The people who let you down are still people who exist in your life in a way I don't. What would it mean to need them anyway, even imperfectly?
